context: Tests whether the agent follows Medusa v2 module architecture patterns: using MedusaService with model-based CRUD generation, defining data models with the model DSL, registering the module with Module(), and wiring it into medusa-config.ts. Also checks correct TypeScript import paths and module encapsulation.

checklist.name: MedusaService extension
checklist.description: Service class extends MedusaService() (called with a model map) rather than a plain class or other base class

checklist.name: model.define() usage
checklist.description: Data model is defined using model.define() rather than a TypeORM entity class or other ORM decorator approach

checklist.name: model DSL field types
checklist.description: Model fields use model.id().primaryKey(), model.text(), model.number(), or model.enum() — not raw TypeScript type annotations or decorators

checklist.name: Module() registration
checklist.description: Module's index.ts exports a default created with Module() from '@medusajs/framework/utils' and specifies the service

checklist.name: Module identifier constant
checklist.description: A named string constant (e.g. WARRANTY_MODULE) is exported from the module index and used as the module key

checklist.name: medusa-config.ts registration
checklist.description: The custom module is listed in the modules array inside medusa-config.ts (or a comment/instruction notes it must be added there)

checklist.name: defineConfig usage
checklist.description: medusa-config.ts uses defineConfig (and optionally loadEnv) imported from '@medusajs/framework/utils'

checklist.name: Module encapsulation
checklist.description: The new domain is implemented as its own module directory (e.g. src/modules/<name>/) containing the service, models, and index rather than scattered files

checklist.name: db:migrate instruction
checklist.description: Output includes mention of running 'npx medusa db:migrate' or equivalent migration step to apply the new model

checklist.name: container.resolve() pattern
checklist.description: Any usage of the module service (e.g. in a route or subscriber) uses container.resolve() rather than a direct import of the service class
